summary: Iter-55 backend: 32/32 pytest assertions pass (27 cross-file regression on test_iter53/54/55 + 5 new in test_iter55_extra.py covering tiffin-prefs/landing upload data-URLs, public /kitchen-location, /admin/payments/cash-totals numeric shape, admin/subscriber gating on /admin/kitchen-settings). Iter-55 frontend: 8/9 features verified live on the preview URL — pre-paint inline theme bootstrap, manifest #ffffff + maskable, SW v3-iter55 SKIP_WAITING + sw-register controllerchange reload, subscriber dashboard payment-stack + LocationPill, profile placeholder 'Enter full name', /admin/cash-analytics (cash-today/month/year/pending-bank tiles + mark-deposited list), /admin/kitchen-settings (lat/lng/radius/address-label inputs + Save). ONE BUG FOUND on iter-55-#4: Checkout.jsx wires the pay-mode-mix tile + state defaults but is MISSING the `data-testid='mix-block'` JSX block with the two mirror-updating number inputs — the user cannot view or edit the online/cash split. Pay-mode tile selects mix correctly and defaults to 50/50, but no UI surfaces to adjust it.

ui_bugs.issue: Selecting pay-mode-mix does NOT reveal the [data-testid='mix-block'] panel with the two number inputs (online_amount + cash_amount). The state hooks (mixOnline/mixCash) and submit-routing to /payments/mix-order exist (lines 77-87, 142-146) but the JSX render block (analogous to the `payMode==='partial'` block at lines 269-294) is missing. Users cannot view or edit the split.

action_items: Add a `{payMode === 'mix' && fees && ( ...mix-block JSX... )}` render block inside Checkout.jsx (around line 295, alongside the partial-down-block) containing two <Input type='number' data-testid='mix-online-input' / 'mix-cash-input'> inputs wrapped in a <div data-testid='mix-block'>. On change of either input, mirror-update the other so online+cash === fees.base. Currently the state hooks default to 50/50 and submission works, but the user has no UI to adjust the split.
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout.jsx state comment at line 22 still says 'online_full | partial | cash' — mix is missing from the comment, which suggests the mix-block UI was inadvertently omitted during this iteration.

Cash analytics tile 'cash-pending-bank' text concatenates the orders-count next to the rupee figure without a separator ('₹4284015 orders' is hard to parse) — consider a line-break or a smaller subtitle for the count.

/api/kitchen-location is public (correct) but currently returns no Cache-Control header — adding `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` would let CDNs cache the 4 fields and improve cold-load of the location pill on the header.
Manifest icons declare only one purpose per entry (good); the maskable PNG should ideally be at least 512x512 with safe-zone padding for Android adaptive icons.
test_credentials: Synthesized via direct DB insert (mirrors tests/test_iter55.py::_make_session): admin user_53fb5454b8c8 (sess_318fa45bc0934a108f8638374b85b106), subscriber user_a1411b1308cb (sess_647ab6811cd7459c9729ed0b51f1e3a3) — both ephemeral in test_database.
seed_data_creation: Two ephemeral users (1 admin, 1 subscriber) + matching user_sessions rows inserted into Mongo `test_database` for the UI walkthrough; can be cleaned via `db.users.delete_many({'user_id': {'$in': ['user_53fb5454b8c8','user_a1411b1308cb']}})`.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green (32/32). The single open bug is the missing mix-block JSX in /app/frontend/src/pages/Checkout.jsx — once the main agent adds the render block with data-testid='mix-block' and two number inputs, retest by: navigate to /checkout/premium_60 with a subscriber session cookie, click [data-testid='pay-mode-mix'], assert [data-testid='mix-block'] is visible, change online input to '100' and assert cash auto-updates so the sum equals plan total (₹2800). Backend /api/payments/mix-order is already verified by test_iter55.py::test_mix_order_*.
rca of the issue: iter-55-#4 mix-block UI: the developer added state hooks (mixOnline, mixCash), the 50/50 init useEffect (lines 80-87), the pay-mode tile (id 'mix', line 256), and the API submit branch (lines 142-146), but skipped wiring the actual JSX panel that exposes the two number inputs. The check `grep -n 'mix-block' /app/frontend/src/pages/Checkout.jsx` returns zero matches. Mitigation: insert a new conditional block immediately after the existing `payMode === 'partial'` block (line 294) — pattern: render a <div data-testid='mix-block'> with two Inputs whose onChange handlers cross-update via `setMixOnline(v); setMixCash(fees.base - v)` and the reverse.
